Let me try moving the blue block to the far left and then up, to see if the up-boundary depends on the horizontal position.
Actually, I think the key insight is that the blue block is blocked from moving up when it's in columns that overlap with the tower. Let me try moving the blue block LEFT so it's completely to the left of the tower, then try moving UP.
The tower is at cols 36-39. The blue block starts at cols 12-31. If I move LEFT by 3 (12 cols), the blue block would be at cols 0-19, which doesn't overlap with the tower. Then I can try moving UP.
Wait, but the blue block started at rows 16-19. When I moved it up earlier, it went to rows 12-15 and then got blocked. But at that point, the blue block was at cols 20-39, which overlaps with the tower at cols 36-39.
So maybe the blue block is blocked from moving up when it overlaps horizontally with the tower. Let me test this by moving the blue block LEFT so it doesn't overlap, then trying UP.
The blue block is at cols 12-31. The tower is at cols 36-39. They don't overlap! So the blue block should be able to move up from the starting position.
Wait, but earlier I moved the blue block UP from rows 16-19 and it went to rows 12-15. Then I tried to move UP again and it was blocked. But at that point, the blue block was at cols 20-39 (after moving RIGHT), which overlaps with the tower.
So the sequence was:
1. Start: rows 16-19, cols 12-31

3. Moved UP 7 times to rows 12-15

But at step 3, the blue block was at cols 12-31, which doesn't overlap with the tower. It moved up from rows 16-19 to 12-15. Why didn't it continue moving up?
Wait, let me re-examine. At step 3, I moved UP 7 times from rows 40-43. Each UP moves 4 rows. 7 UP moves = 28 rows up. 40 - 28 = 12. So the blue block ended at rows 12-15.
But the blue block started at rows 16-19. So it moved up past the starting position to rows 12-15. Why was it blocked at rows 12-15?
Maybe there's a ceiling at row 12 that applies regardless of horizontal position. Or maybe the blue block can only move up to a certain number of steps from its current position.
Actually, I think the ceiling might be at row 8 or so, and the blue block is 4 rows tall. So the top of the blue block can go up to row 8, meaning the blue block occupies rows 8-11. But when I tried to move UP from rows 12-15, it didn't move. This suggests the ceiling is at row 12.
Hmm, but the blue block started at rows 16-19. If the ceiling is at row 12, then the blue block can move up from 16-19 to 12-15, but not further. That would mean the ceiling is at row 12.
But wait, the charcoal block is at rows 1-3. If the ceiling is at row 12, the blue block can never reach the charcoal block.
Let me try a different approach. Let me move the blue block LEFT so it's completely to the left of the tower, then try moving UP from the starting position.
